decisions: Created all core memory files as empty templates (MISTAKES.md, PREFERENCES.md, LEARNINGS.md) — no prior MEMORY.md existed to migrate from, confirmed by checking both brain repo root and auto-memory path
Set SUMMARY.md identity from CLAUDE.md identity section ('Smith — StepForge Workflow Agent') since no prior memory entries existed to consolidate
observations: This is a brand new brain with zero prior memory — all tiers are empty
CLAUDE.md had no @ imports before this migration; now includes @memory/SUMMARY.md and @memory/TODAY.md
No MEMORY.md file existed in either the brain repo or auto-memory directory, so Migration A was purely structural (directory creation + empty templates)
recommendations: After first real user interactions, verify that daily log entries are being written to memory/TODAY.md continuously
Monitor that core/MISTAKES.md and core/PREFERENCES.md accumulate entries as the agent interacts — empty core files after 5+ sessions would indicate the routing logic isn't triggering
processImprovements: [self-critique] This is the first maintenance run — no prior process to critique. The main risk is that future sessions may not write to TODAY.md consistently since there's no established habit yet.
[proposal] On the next consolidation, check git log for any regular-session commits and verify each one has a corresponding TODAY.md entry. This would catch the 'forgot to log' anti-pattern early.
